description: Synchronize all Miss Lana project canon and operational documentation with the newly locked public brand spelling, primary domain, email, contact workflow, and current migration status. Replace obsolete project-owned references to 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theater' and misslanatheater.com with the locked 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre' and misslanatheatre.com, while preserving third-party proper names, historical URLs, quoted search phrases, and other contexts where 'theater' is intentionally correct. This task changes documentation canon only; it must not redesign pages, rewrite unrelated marketing copy, or modify production code.

primary_authorities: Current operator decision in project chat dated 2026-06-25
current_truth: The locked public brand is 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre'.
The short umbrella name remains 'Miss Lana'.
The primary production domain is misslanatheatre.com.
The production site has already been moved to misslanatheatre.com; documentation must not describe this domain as merely available, proposed, or awaiting purchase.
The primary public email is info@misslanatheatre.com.
The second phone number is reserve-only and must not be presented as an equal primary contact.
Customers may contact the theatre by SMS, email, or WhatsApp; any written method is preferred.
Svitlana currently answers inquiries herself; there is no manager or backup operator.
The realistic reply window is 1-2 business days.
Messages may be sent at any time; calls should be placed during reasonable daytime hours.
Customers do not need a full emailed copy of the submitted inquiry, but the website should display a clear submission confirmation.
Approved confirmation copy: 'Thank you! We've received your request. Miss Lana will reply by text, email, or WhatsApp within 1-2 business days.'
misslanatheater.com may be retained only as a protective alternate domain and should 301 redirect to misslanatheatre.com.
magic-castle-puppet-theater.com remains a legacy domain and should 301 redirect to the most relevant new URLs.
The website remains one English-first site with four service lines under Miss Lana: Fairy-Tale Theatre, Birthday Parties, School Shows, and Miss Lana & Friends / Characters.

The business remains a professional travelling live children's theatre based in Los Angeles, with four performers, 30+ years of experience primarily in Ukraine, live costumed fairy-tale performances, interactive activities, ages 2-10, and a starting public price of $350 unless later owner decisions change it.
The brand is not positioned as Russian, Slavic, or puppet-first.
Trademark clearance remains required before heavy investment in final logo, print, merchandise, or permanent branded production assets. Domain migration does not equal trademark clearance.
precedence_rules: The current operator decision in this task overrides older brand/domain/contact statements in the listed project documents.
After synchronization, BRAND.md is the source of truth for brand name, domain, email naming, and service-line naming.
PROJECT_BRIEF.md remains the source of truth for business facts.
Do not alter verified third-party names, official venue names, competitor names, quoted titles, or historical facts merely to force Theatre spelling.

Do not replace 'theater' inside legacy domains, source URLs, quotations, file names, schema vocabulary, code identifiers, or SEO keyword examples unless the context is project-owned branding and the change is explicitly correct.
allowed_change_classes: Status-label updates such as proposed/available to live/primary
forbidden_change_classes: DNS, email, hosting, redirect, or deployment changes
Unconfirmed safety, insurance, background-check, or school-document claims
Unrelated rewriting of show descriptions, design tokens, or business strategy

prerequisites.blocking_condition: If tung_system_specification.md is missing from the repository, do not invent or reconstruct the canonical specification. Report the missing prerequisite, but continue the documentation synchronization only if the executor already has an approved v2-full runtime/template that validates this task.

in_scope: Audit all listed project documents for obsolete project-owned brand spelling, domain, email, phone, contact workflow, and migration status.
Update the public brand to 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre' everywhere it refers to this project or its own service line.
Update the primary domain to misslanatheatre.com and primary email to info@misslanatheatre.com.
Update documentation to state that the production site is already on the new domain.
Update contact guidance to one primary phone, reserve-only second phone, SMS/email/WhatsApp availability, Svitlana as sole responder, and 1-2 business-day response window.
Update form and contact-block specifications to include preferred written contact methods and the approved on-screen confirmation message.
Update GBP, schema-name, metadata-name, footer, header, contact, migration, and SEO documentation references where they describe project-owned branding.
Preserve both 'theatre' and 'theater' in SEO research where they represent distinct real user queries; mark Theatre as the public brand spelling.
Add concise changelog/status notes to core files indicating the 2026-06-25 canon sync.
Produce an audit report of changed files, preserved exceptions, unresolved questions, and verification results.
out_of_scope: Editing the live website or application code.
Implementing redirects, DNS, email mailboxes, form delivery, GBP, analytics, schema, or Search Console.
Writing the final Home page copy deck.
Resolving open owner questions about travel surcharges, insurance, deposits, cancellations, show names, or public-school paperwork.
Changing official names of competitors or venues such as Bob Baker Marionette Theater.
Replacing American search-query examples solely for stylistic consistency.
Editing deprecated TUNG v1 or TUNG specifications beyond correcting project-specific Miss Lana examples, if any.

non_goals: Do not perform a global blind replacement of every occurrence of 'theater'.
Do not redesign the website or alter design-system tokens.
Do not invent missing business facts or close owner questions without evidence.
Do not treat the domain migration as trademark clearance.
Do not make the reserve phone publicly equivalent to the primary phone.
Do not create a second public brand spelling or mixed Theater/Theatre identity.
Do not modify third-party proper names, quoted material, historical URLs, or source citations.
Do not remove valid SEO keyword variants that contain 'theater'.
forbidden_execution_patterns: No repository-wide search-and-replace without per-occurrence semantic review.
No changing files outside the declared scope merely because they contain the word theater.
No silent deletion of historical context; obsolete decisions should be updated or clearly marked superseded where useful.
No claiming redirects, email delivery, DNS, GBP, Search Console, or website code are configured unless separately verified.
No replacing legacy-domain strings with the new domain inside migration instructions.
No changing competitor or venue spellings to match the Miss Lana brand.
No publishing unconfirmed contact hours as exact opening hours.
No converting the 1-2 business-day response window into a faster promise.
No broad copy rewrite disguised as canon synchronization.
No completion claim without a post-edit residue scan and manual exception review.
execution_guardrails: Create a pre-edit occurrence inventory for: Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theater, misslanatheater.com, info@misslanatheater.com, 213-282-1054, 323-903-2039, proposed/available domain wording, and mixed Theater/Theatre service-line names.

Classify every occurrence before editing as PROJECT_BRAND, PROJECT_DOMAIN, PROJECT_EMAIL, PROJECT_CONTACT, SEO_KEYWORD, THIRD_PARTY_NAME, LEGACY_URL, QUOTATION, HISTORICAL_NOTE, or TECHNICAL_IDENTIFIER.
Automatically change only PROJECT_BRAND, PROJECT_DOMAIN, PROJECT_EMAIL, and confirmed PROJECT_CONTACT occurrences; manually inspect all other classes.
Use ASCII apostrophe in technical/domain-adjacent contexts and preserve typographic apostrophe only where the existing documentation style intentionally uses it.
Keep the domain lowercase exactly as misslanatheatre.com and email lowercase exactly as info@misslanatheatre.com.
Use the formatted phone exactly as (323) 903-2039 in public-copy specifications and tel:+13239032039 where a technical link example is required.
Document the second phone as reserve-only; if its exact number remains in old documentation, remove it from public-facing specifications unless an explicit alternate-contact label is required.
Preserve the old domain magic-castle-puppet-theater.com exactly in migration and redirect references.
Preserve misslanatheater.com only in protective-domain and redirect context after synchronization.
Keep SEO keyword examples such as children's theater Los Angeles when they reflect American search intent; add theatre variants where useful, but do not dilute the locked public spelling.
Do not alter design principles, colors, fonts, age range, duration, pricing, team size, or service architecture unless required to correct a direct contradiction with current truth.
For every modified file, review headings, tables, code fences, internal links, examples, and changelog text—not only body paragraphs.
Use git diff or equivalent full diff review before closure.
All changed Markdown files must remain valid UTF-8 and readable.

steps.title: Establish repository paths and create a pre-edit inventory
actions: Locate the canonical copies of all required project documents; do not assume the uploaded flat paths equal repository paths.
Record git status before changes.
Run case-sensitive and case-insensitive searches for obsolete brand/domain/email/contact strings and migration-status wording.
Create docs/reports/MISS_LANA_CANON_SYNC_001_pre_edit_inventory.md with file, line, occurrence, classification, and intended action.
steps.expected_outcome: Every relevant occurrence is known and semantically classified before editing.

steps.title: Update the brand lock first
actions: Edit BRAND.md before all dependent files.
Lock 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre', misslanatheatre.com, and info@misslanatheatre.com.
State that misslanatheatre.com is already the primary production domain.
State that misslanatheater.com is protective/alternate only and should 301 to the primary domain.
Retain the one-site/four-service-lines architecture and trademark-clearance gate.
Add a 2026-06-25 changelog/status note explaining that Theatre now replaces Theater in project-owned branding.
steps.expected_outcome: BRAND.md becomes the updated source of truth before dependent documentation is changed.

steps.title: Synchronize business facts and project instructions
actions: Update PROJECT_BRIEF.md with the locked brand, domain, email, production-domain status, primary phone, reserve-phone rule, written contact options, response owner, and 1-2 business-day response window.
Update 00_PROJECT_INSTRUCTIONS.md and 05_BUILD_CLAUDE.md so future agents read the new canon and do not reintroduce Theater or misslanatheater.com as primary.
Preserve all unrelated business facts and negative-scope rules.
steps.expected_outcome: Human and agent-facing project instructions agree with the brand lock and current contact workflow.

steps.title: Synchronize positioning, content, structure, and design documentation
actions: Update project-owned brand and service-line spelling in 01_CONTENT_INVENTORY.md, 02_POSITIONING_AND_TONE.md, 03_SITEMAP_AND_SCOPE.md, DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, SITE_STRUCTURE_AND_BLOCKS.md, and 2026-06-22-design-system-research-v2.md.
Update header/footer/contact/form specifications to use the primary phone and new email.
Update the Booking/LeadForm specification with SMS, email, WhatsApp, optional phone call, Svitlana as sole responder, and approved success-state copy.
Mark the second phone as reserve-only and remove it from equal public presentation.
Preserve design tokens, IA, show facts, and unrelated recommendations.
steps.expected_outcome: All content, UX, and design specifications use one public spelling and current contact facts.

steps.title: Synchronize SEO and migration documentation without damaging search intent
actions: Update 04_SEO.md so the public brand, GBP business name, schema name, canonical domain, primary email references, and migration status use Theatre/misslanatheatre.com.
State that the website is already on the new domain while redirect and listing verification remain implementation/verification work.
Retain misslanatheater.com only as an alternate redirect domain.
Retain magic-castle-puppet-theater.com as the legacy domain to redirect.
Preserve American-search keyword examples containing theater; add paired theatre variants only where editorially useful.
Do not claim redirects or Search Console updates are completed unless repository evidence proves it.
steps.expected_outcome: SEO documentation supports one public brand while retaining valid search-language variants and migration history.

steps.title: Perform residue scan and semantic exception review
actions: Search all in-scope documents again for obsolete brand/domain/email references.
For every remaining occurrence of 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theater', misslanatheater.com, or info@misslanatheater.com, document why it is intentionally preserved or correct it.
Review every remaining standalone 'theater' occurrence in context and classify it as third-party, SEO, legacy, quotation, historical, technical, or error.
Search for obsolete statements that the new domain is free, proposed, awaiting purchase, or not yet live.
Search for two-phone equal presentation and obsolete email/contact flow.
Confirm no official third-party names or old URLs were corrupted.
steps.expected_outcome: No unexplained obsolete project-owned references remain.

steps.title: Review diffs and create synchronization report
actions: Review the complete diff file by file for scope drift, accidental replacements, broken Markdown, and factual changes.

Create docs/reports/MISS_LANA_CANON_SYNC_001_report.md with executive summary, changed files, key decisions applied, preserved Theater exceptions, unresolved owner questions, verification commands, and final status.
Create docs/reviews/MISS_LANA_CANON_SYNC_001_review.md with reviewer-grade analysis of consistency, accidental edits, unresolved contradictions, and recommendations.
Update project progress tracking only if the repository already has a canonical progress file; do not invent a new progress system.
steps.expected_outcome: The canon sync is documented, reviewable, and safe to hand to the next website-copy task.
affected_files: Existing canonical progress file if present

success_criteria: All project-owned brand references use 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre'.
Documentation states that the production site is already on misslanatheatre.com.
misslanatheater.com appears only in protective-domain/redirect context.
magic-castle-puppet-theater.com remains intact in legacy migration context.
The primary public phone is (323) 903-2039 and the second number is not presented as an equal primary contact.
SMS, email, and WhatsApp are documented as accepted written channels.
Svitlana is documented as the current sole responder with a realistic 1-2 business-day response window.
The booking specification includes a clear on-screen confirmation and does not require sending the customer a full copy of the inquiry.
Third-party proper names, official venue names, quotations, legacy URLs, and valid SEO query examples remain correct.
No blind global replacement damage exists.
No unrelated strategy, pricing, design, show, or code changes were introduced.
Pre-edit inventory, final report, and reviewer-grade review files exist and are readable.
The final residue scan contains no unexplained obsolete project-owned references.

required_commands: git diff -- BRAND.md PROJECT_BRIEF.md 00_PROJECT_INSTRUCTIONS.md 01_CONTENT_INVENTORY.md 02_POSITIONING_AND_TONE.md 03_SITEMAP_AND_SCOPE.md 04_SEO.md DESIGN_SYSTEM.md SITE_STRUCTURE_AND_BLOCKS.md 05_BUILD_CLAUDE.md 2026-06-22-design-system-research-v2.md
manual_checks: Confirm every remaining misslanatheater.com reference is explicitly protective/redirect context.
Confirm every remaining project-relevant Theater occurrence is either a valid SEO keyword, third-party proper name, quotation, legacy URL, historical note, or technical term.
Confirm the old legacy domain remains unchanged.
Confirm no exact business hours were invented from the phrase reasonable daytime hours.
Confirm the response promise remains 1-2 business days.
Confirm the second phone is not displayed as equal primary contact in any public-facing specification.
Confirm the approved success message is copied exactly or changed only for punctuation/typography without changing meaning.
Confirm all Markdown tables and code fences remain valid and readable.
Confirm no production implementation claims were added without evidence.
Confirm all non_goals, forbidden_execution_patterns, and execution_guardrails were respected.
failure_conditions: Any unexplained obsolete project-owned brand/domain/email reference remains.
Any third-party name, legacy URL, or search-query example is incorrectly rewritten.
Any production code or deployment configuration is modified.
Any new unsupported business fact is introduced.
Any required report or review file is missing.
The executor cannot access the canonical v2 specification or approved v2 template required by repository policy.

report_requirements: List every modified file and summarize the exact classes of changes.
List all intentionally preserved Theater occurrences by category, not necessarily every identical SEO-keyword repetition.
List unresolved owner questions without attempting to solve them.
State whether the repository contains and validates against tung_system_specification.md.
Include command results or concise pass/fail summaries.
Declare whether the task is READY FOR NEXT TASK or BLOCKED.
final_response_requirements: State the task status: completed, partially completed, or blocked.
Summarize preserved exceptions and unresolved questions.
Do not claim website code, redirects, email delivery, or GBP were implemented.

closure_sequence: Step 1 — Scope verification: confirm only declared documentation and report files were changed; confirm no non_goals were implemented.
Step 2 — Canon verification: confirm BRAND.md, PROJECT_BRIEF.md, and all dependent docs consistently use Theatre, misslanatheatre.com, info@misslanatheatre.com, and the approved contact workflow.
Step 3 — Exception verification: review all preserved Theater occurrences and confirm each is third-party, SEO, legacy, quotation, historical, or technical context.
Step 4 — Reviewer-grade review: inspect the full diff for accidental replacement, contradictions, broken links/tables/fences, overreach, and unsupported claims; save the review artifact.
Step 5 — Command verification: run every required verification command and resolve all failures within scope.
Step 6 — Deliverable verification: confirm all required modified/created files exist, are readable, and contain the required content.
Step 7 — Execution-safety confirmation: explicitly confirm no non_goals were fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns were violated, and all execution_guardrails were respected.
Step 8 — Closure statement: declare READY FOR NEXT TASK only if all success criteria pass; otherwise declare BLOCKED and list exact blockers without pretending completion.

post_actions: Do not edit website code as part of this task.
After successful closure, use the synchronized canon as the only source for the next task: site-wide public copy inventory and Home/contact/form text synchronization.
Create a separate TUNG v2-full task for production website text/code changes.
Create separate implementation tasks for domain redirects, email delivery, GBP, Search Console, schema, and form handling if not already verified.
